rationale.R1.1: A general section review is expected to consider whether the thesis and contribution framing are coherent at the section level.
rationale.R1.2: The reviewer would be expected to check whether the argument from reported results to conclusions is logically valid.
rationale.R1.3: The section makes empirical claims that should be checked against the reported numbers and comparisons.
rationale.R1.6: A request to review a section centrally includes sentence- and paragraph-level clarity, flow, and register.
rationale.RX.1: The projection must preserve uncertainty about what kind of review was intended by the short delegation.
rationale.RX.2: The section contains strong claims such as eliminating bias, so avoiding overclaiming is an active responsibility.
rationale.RX.3: The agent must stay within reviewing the section rather than rewriting or expanding the artifact.
rationale.RX.4: The review should avoid enabling misleading research claims or unsupported deployment implications.
rationale.RX.5: Any projected responsibility should remain traceable to the delegation and the artifact text.
